title: Whering AI outfit quality is its weakest point — explainable looks are AWEAR wedge
summary: Multiple independent sources describe Whering Dress Me/AI looks as near-random: "not much better than grabbing clothes at random", "disjointed from your personal style", "basic combinations rather than intelligent recommendations based on color harmony". No competitor shows WHY a look works.
evidence: Indyx comparison + 2026 roundups (beautyai/aurelle) + stylewithingrace tester review
proposal: Make look explainability a visible differentiator: in AI stylist / Todays Look, show why-this-works chips (color harmony, occasion, weather) next to the % match. Additive to existing Todays Look card.

title: Cataloging friction is the category make-or-break; Whering wins with auto bg-removal, loses on tag errors
summary: Whering top praise = auto background removal + auto-tagging ("removes the background & resizes the photos"); top frustration = tagging errors ("quite frustrating when Whering gets it wrong") and upload glitches. SCAN onboarding quality decides retention in this category.
evidence: whering.co.uk homepage + Indyx + review sentiment aggregate
proposal: Benchmark AWEAR SCAN flow vs Whering: auto bg-removal + auto-tag + one-tap tag correction UX; define and measure time-to-first-10-items as the SCAN onboarding metric.

title: Weather-aware daily outfit suggestion is table stakes — AWEAR has the infra, not the wiring
summary: Whering planner suggests daily outfits based on wardrobe + weather. AWEAR already has weather infrastructure (per in.md) and a Todays Look occasion engine (pickOccasionForNow) — weather is not wired into it.
evidence: 2026 roundups on Whering planner; AWEAR in.md source catalog notes weather infra exists
proposal: Wire existing weather data into pickOccasionForNow so Todays Look adapts to real weather (rain/heat) in addition to day-of-week + hour. Additive, no rework of the shipped card.

title: Whering reputation dragged by crashes/glitches — reliability is a differentiator in this category
summary: Review sentiment: "waaay too glitchy", "actually unusable" after updates, crashes during upload, slow performance. The category leader is beatable on quality bar alone; a crash-free/perf gate on mobile releases converts this into durable advantage.
evidence: Review sentiment aggregate (justuseapp/kimola snippets via WebSearch; direct fetch 403)
proposal: Adopt a crash-free / performance gate before mobile releases (Metro bundle + smoke flow on closet upload). Process change, fully reversible.

title: Whering monetization: free core, reported ~GBP 4.99/mo premium, brand contests; sustainability questioned
summary: Homepage says free to manage and style your closet; one 2025/26 roundup reports premium at ~GBP 4.99/month (UNVERIFIED vs official source). Brand collabs referenced (Prada, Gucci, Coperni, Vivienne Westwood). Indyx flags "the user is the product". Claims 7M users. Relevant price anchor for any future AWEAR premium (e.g. AI-look quota).
evidence: whering.co.uk + Indyx + roundup aggregate; App Store fetch rate-limited (429)
proposal: Founder decision: use ~GBP 4.99/mo as the freemium price anchor when defining AWEAR premium (AI-look quota already parked in IDEAS #35). Strategic/economic — not for scout to act alone.

title: Whering has no closed commerce loop — AWEAR BUY/EARN is unoccupied ground
summary: Indyx: no integrated resale marketplace in Whering; one roundup mentions a Vestiaire Collective integration (CONFLICTING, unresolved). Neither source shows in-app purchase or seller-earn loops. AWEAR already shipped /api/orders + preloved commission — this is the positioning flank.
evidence: Indyx comparison vs roundup mention of Vestiaire — conflict documented in doc
proposal: Founder-level positioning: lean marketing/product narrative on the closed loop (scan->match->look->buy preloved->earn) that Whering does not close. Strategic/brand — not for scout to act alone.
